Charlie Austin, currently playing for QPR, is under consideration for a surprise call-up to the England national team due to his impressive goal-scoring streak for the club. Former England captain Roy Hodgson, despite having several top strikers, is interested in Austin's abilities. Though QPR faces relegation, Austin's performance indicates that he may join the senior side as they prepare for upcoming matches against the Republic of Ireland and Serbia in the Euro 2016 qualification process. His potential inclusion comes at a time when England's squad could be reshuffled before their upcoming match against Brazil.

Game of Thrones fans are pirating the popular HBO series in record numbers because the show's creator David Beaton recently announced that the upcoming fifth season would air simultaneously around the world instead of just America. Pirates use the argument that delayed American releases allow them to catch up with similar content from other nations but claim that piracy can't be stopped. HBO responded to the situation by launching an online streaming platform called HBO Now, though it is only available in America at this point.

In an interview with Canal Plus, Juventus defender Patrice Evra recounted how Sir Alex Ferguson encouraged him to consider becoming a coach someday during his tenure at Manchester United. Evra was impressed enough by Ferguson to receive some coaching credentials from the legendary manager before leaving for Juventus in 2014 after eight years at the club, according to Evra. He hopes to pass some more coaching badges before doing so. Evra also mentioned that Ferguson previously told him, along with other former teammates like Ryan Giggs, that they both have the potential to become great coaches.

A new study from researchers at the University of Southern California finds that there is a gene responsible for determining vulnerability to noise-induced hearing loss. This gene, called Nox3, is located in the inner ear and plays a crucial role in hearing function. The finding could lead to personalized prevention strategies for hearing loss, especially among individuals who are more genetically predisposed to it.

Susie Clark from Arkansas found a 3.69 carat diamond in Arkansas at the Crater of Diamonds State Park. Clark says she prayed for a diamond when she first visited the park 33 years ago. Clark claims the diamond helped her find it and called it the Hallelujah diamond. It was the largest diamond found in the park in 2018. Clark plans to keep the diamond. Park officials say the plowing fields help bring diamonds to the surface.

The current chief inspector of child protection, Brett Anthony O'Connor, is appealing against a ban imposed on the press to name him as a suspect in a string of alleged crimes committed decades ago. A judge refused the request on Monday, saying it could cause psychological trauma or embarrassment. While his lawyer successfully asked for anonymity, authorities have agreed to withhold information about the victim(s). An investigation found O'Connor guilty of engaging in indecent assaults towards two teenage girls in 1987 and again in 1989. O'Connor, previously identified as a priest, now faces charges related to five other allegations. His case continues in another courtroom.

In 2008, goalkeeper David Seaman saved Sheffield United's only goal in a match that decided the winner of the 1997 FA Cup Final between Arsenal and Chelsea. Seaman's dramatic stoppage kick became one of the most memorable moments in cup history. This victory came shortly before Seaman himself retired due to a shoulder injury, making the save particularly significant. Seaman reflects that the 2011 FA Cup semi-final will hold no less importance as he looks ahead to facing another tough opponent in Reading. Despite their recent achievements, Seaman urges Arsenal to remain cautious and not let their momentum falter.

Burger King offered to cover the expenses and provide gifts for the wedding of Joel and Ashley. Joel and Ashley have been known as Burger-King since elementary school. Burger King planned to personalize the wedding experience with gift bags, mason jars, and burger king crowns. Burger King hopes that Joel and Ashley will continue to love each other forever.

A mysterious man claiming to suffer from severe memory loss was found in Poland. Local authorities released a photo and description hoping locals would recognize him. His accent, however, remains unknown. Despite wearing clothes that suggest a connection to Europe, his origins remain unknown. This case highlights the challenges faced by law enforcement in attempting to identify individuals based on their language skills alone. The man, whose identity is still undetermined, continues to reside in a psychiatric facility awaiting further developments in his condition.

Raheem Sterling, a talented young footballer, expressed uncertainty about his future at Liverpool despite being offered a significant salary increase. He revealed that he would only have agreed to the new contract had it been significantly lower. Despite his talents, Sterling has struggled to maintain his form from the previous season, scoring fewer goals and improving statistics compared to when he was playing for Liverpool. This situation highlights Sterling's challenges in staying consistent and proving himself at Liverpool.

A massive fire broke out at an old building in Flint, Michigan, where a strip club called The Body Shop used to operate. The building caught fire at around noon on Wednesday, causing damage but no fatalities or injuries. Authorities have launched an investigation into what caused the fire. The incident occurred along a Flint street, where another fire had been reported before, leading residents to think this might not be an isolated occurrence. No one has died from the fire so far, but emergency services responded quickly to extinguish the blaze. The situation remains under scrutiny, with firefighters continuing their investigation.

Ashley Young, one of Manchester United's top wingers, took advantage of an injury to Michael Carrick to score twice against Manchester City in the Premier League. He helped set up fellow player Marouane Fellaini before scoring himself. His teammate Juan Mata added a third goal, sending the match to penalties. Young praised the "noisy neighbors" of Manchester City as loud supporters at Old Trafford and believes his team is capable of winning any game if they stay focused.

Back pain has become more common among teenagers and young adults, according to surveys and doctors' reports. Factors contributing include increased screen time (especially on smartphones), poor posture due to extended use of handheld devices, and the trend toward "hot desking." Doctors worry this could lead to an earlier onset of spinal issues like kyphosis, and recommend taking frequent breaks to alleviate back strain. Back pain is so prevalent that surveys indicate around half of those between 16 and 30 report having back or neck pain at least once per week.

David Villa continues to support his former club Atlético Madrid despite leaving them behind when he moved to Major League Soccer's New York City FC. During the quarter-final first-leg match against Real Madrid, Villa was seen cheering enthusiastically for Atlético during the game, where he helped prevent a goal from being scored due to a missed opportunity by Real Madrid's star player, Gareth Bale. Despite this setback, New York City FC has become Villa's new home, and he recently scored two goals in a victory over New England Revolution. Villa is now expected to be part of a future partnership between Atlético Madrid and former England internationals like Frank Lampard joining him in New York.

A dad was trying to get his kids to try sushi, only to watch them run away from the roll, so his daughter stepped up and stuffed it in her mouth without thinking, winning her father's prize money. The video went viral and many people thought it was cute and even some said they hated sushi like the kids did.

A 6-year-old girl named Nurhayada Sofia fell to her death from an escalator at a Malaysian shopping center. She was shopping with her mother and sister when something went wrong and she became trapped under the moving escalator. Her mother tried calling her husband but could not reach him before she died. The incident caused chaos among bystanders and the family members involved, leading to an immediate investigation and condolences from everyone around her.

In a bizarre incident during a match between Barcelona and Celta Vigo, Celta player Fabian Orellana threw grass at his opponent, causing severe injury to Barcelona player Sergio Busquets. Orellana was subsequently sent off for the match and fined €2 million. Orellana then missed his team's next match as a result. The incident resulted in Barcelona securing a win with goals from Dani Alves and Jeremy Mathieu.

Fiorentina defeated Sampdoria 2-0 thanks to Alessandro Diamanti's and Mohamed Salah's goals, raising their league position to 49 points. Meanwhile, Roma secured a home victory over Napoli with Miralem Pjanic's goal, while AC Milan earned their first away win in five months with a 2-1 defeat of Palermo. Additionally, Juventus managed to beat Empoli 2-0 despite facing adversity with players' unpaid wages. Cesena drew with Verona 3-3 and remain third-bottom on 22 points, trailing Atalanta by one.

Amanda Berry, an 18-year-old woman from the United Kingdom, was upset that she was bombarded with inappropriate texts from a male representative of a telecommunications company while on the telephone. The man identified himself as "Miguel" but used various names and images throughout his communication to make it clear he was attempting to seduce her, despite her initial attempts at conversation. Despite these efforts, Berry believes that the man continues to harass her. Berry is reported to be seeking legal advice in order to address the situation. Her case highlights issues around personal privacy and consent during interactions with corporations or individuals online.

In this article, it is reported that the British Army spent approximately £200 million in ammunition alone during the eight-year war with the Taliban in Afghanistan. The total number of bullets fired was around 46 million. Around half of those bullets were fired using the SA-80 assault rifle and the other half using the minimi machine gun. This shows the fierce combat conditions faced by both sides. It is also noted that around 2 million artillery shells costing £1,250 each were fired during the conflict. The helicopter guns shot over 80,000 rounds at Taliban targets. Overall, the article stresses the intensity and danger of the fighting during the time period.

A 50-year-old man named Zhang died in a road accident that happened eight months ago near his home. It seems that the tragedy that caused his death also struck a blow to another member of their family. Their brother was killed in an accident involving a big truck nearly identical to the one that caused his brother's death. The older brother, Zhang, suffered fatal injuries during the collision and died in the hospital. An eyewitness claims that the driver fled the scene in a similar color of car without stopping. Authorities say that more details about what happened will come out soon.

Firefighter Cody Knecht used a noisy ringing phone attached to a stick pole he held over the storm drain to attract six duckling chicks inside. He had to dive deep into the water and use a torch to retrieve one duckling before calming down enough to grab another. The remaining four were found at their home several hours later.

An 11-year-old boy named Shiva allegedly committed theft and then faced severe physical abuse from his teacher at Dwarika Prasad School in Railamau village, India. A teacher admitted hitting Shiva during interrogation. His body later died in hospital due to fatal internal bleeding. This incident highlights serious concerns regarding bullying within educational institutions and raises questions about appropriate disciplinary measures against such conduct.

Charlie Austin made a significant error on his first day at Queens Park Rangers, mistaking Joey Barton for someone else, but ultimately realized his mistake and became a good friend. In an interview, Austin recalled Barton's initial reaction, which was surprising but understandable given the circumstances. After Barton's second meeting with Austin, the duo developed a strong bond, leading Austin to compare himself favorably to strikers Harry Kane, Diego Costa, and Sergio Aguero during a debate with his girlfriend. Despite being compared unfavorably to these top scorers, Austin's goal-scoring record has proven impressive, placing him third among active players in terms of total goals scored this season. His upcoming match against West Brom represents another opportunity for Austin to prove his worth as a player.

A nine-year-old boy was abused by his adoptive mother because he didn't know the story of Pinocchio. The mother was found guilty of abusing the boy and is being held in custody. It's important that families take care of their children properly so this doesn't happen again.

The 1960s hit band, later found out to be Jimmy Savile, attended a famous paedophile brothel in Battersea in 1964, according to a secret police report. A woman claimed that Savile abused her there in the 1970s when he appeared on "Top of the Pops." The police investigation into the brothel started in 2013 after details about Savile's abuse surfaced. The connection between the band and Savile remains unclear, as the incident did not occur simultaneously, but both were associated with the same establishment.

A six-year-old child praised then-senator Barack Obama as "too young" for his age during a question-and-answer session with President-Elect Hillary Clinton, prompting him to request that the little girl come onstage to receive an enthusiastic hug.

In an interview with Sky Sports, PSV Eindhoven midfielder Geinogio Wijnaldum expressed his desire to play in a "great club" in a major European league, particularly mentioning the possibility of moving to the Premier League. Despite being content with PSV Eindhoven, where he has scored 16 goals this season, Wijnaldum is influenced by the appeal of playing in bigger clubs with top-level talent in England, Italy, Spain, Germany, and France. His comments suggest he is considering leaving Netherlands to pursue greater opportunities.

The British Army's newly formed 77 Brigade is struggling to find enough technical experts to combat Islamic State's dominance over the internet and social media. Despite its initial promise, the brigade numbers only 454 regular and reservist troops. Brigadier Aitken acknowledges difficulties in recruiting 'talent' and notes a lack of diverse viewpoints, adding that the brigade may need to re-design its terms of service to attract individuals with specialized skills. Some reservists may lose their positions, leading to a mutinous atmosphere at the brigade's headquarters. The Ministry of Defence announced that those losing their posts will receive alternative roles.

In eastern China, a farmer named Xiao Qibin had an unexpected surprise - his newborn goat was born with six legs instead of the usual four. After discovering this unique birth defect, people have nicknamed the six-legged goat "Spider Goat". Despite being born with some challenges, the goat is doing well and has even gained popularity among locals for its unusual appearance. While farmers haven't yet attempted to surgically remove the extra limbs, they plan to let nature take its course, keeping the goat healthy and happy in its natural environment.

A man died after being crushed by a garbage truck that rolled forward and trapped him in Melbourne's west. Another man died and two others remained hospitalized after a car filled with six people crashed in Melbourne's east. The drivers of both incidents were reportedly unharmed.

Scott Stephenson, a 19-year-old defendant, committed the crime of theft while lying in a coma, with no one around to call for help. He then ignored bystanders' reactions and engaged in an offensive verbal tirade just minutes before facing legal consequences for his actions.

Former England footballer Harry Redknapp faced physical abuse and verbal attacks from Arsenal supporters after an away match between Arsenal and Liverpool. He claims that fans attacked him with coins and verbally abused him as he left the stadium. Redknapp previously served two spells as a coach for Arsenal rival Tottenham Hotspur, which he considers unfair treatment. The incident highlights the volatile nature of the matchday experience among football fans.

Per Mertesacker, the "arguing culture" he brings back to Arsenal could play a significant role in improving their performance and mentality. After some heavy losses during the previous season, the team faced heavy defeats against Manchester City and Liverpool. Mertesacker believes that having tough conversations helps them focus better and understand why they lost. Despite recent improvements, they still need to work together closely as a unit to avoid embarrassing defeats again.

In an apparent case of mistaken identity, young woman Miranda Ashley Calvin was arrested for allegedly hitting and fleeing with her car after striking and killing 49-year-old Jerrie Ann Hornig while the latter crossed the street. Calvins phone rang shortly before the accident occurred, prompting her to flee the scene on foot - her movements quickly identified as suspicious behavior. Hornigs son Maxwell Horning has since posted photos on social media, expressing deep sorrow over the loss of his mother and warning others to stay alert in such situations. His tribute and photograph of her from high school help highlight the emotional impact of what happened.

Martin Skrtel, a Liverpool defender who has been linked with a transfer to Wolfsburg or Napoli, has stated that he will remain with the club until his contract expires in 2016. The Slovakia national team player has made over 200 league appearances for the club since joining in 2008 and denies any rumors of a transfer to Germany or Italy. He has reportedly been negotiating an extension to his current contract with Liverpool's management.

On Saturday morning in Orlando, Florida, a 20-year-old woman named Britney E. Montenegro became involved in a brawl between multiple parties within a bar. As the fight spread onto the street, Montenegro was restrained but suffered injuries including cuts that others had. Police arrested her later, claiming she did not cause them herself, although they said they found evidence that someone else might be responsible. Despite some bruising, Montenegro reportedly only had minor scrapes, though one person identified the location where the incident began. The altercation drew attention to public safety concerns related to alcohol consumption in crowded areas.

Brazilian soccer player Lucas Moura expressed admiration for Lionel Messi, calling him "the best player in the world" despite being from Argentina. Moura believes Messi's technique and style of play make him superior. Despite this rivalry, both players agree that competition within their own country, Brazil, should not overshadow international rivalries like those involving Messi or Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) vs. Barcelona. Moura hopes his team, Paris Saint-Germain, can secure a win over Barcelona in their upcoming Champions League match.

Eligah Christian, who was out on a felony warrant for allegedly stealing money, fled from authorities after trying to drive away from them in a motorhome. Christian attempted to get away by driving recklessly through Alaska state troopers. After a high-speed chase that involved the troopers hitting several of Christian's vehicles, Christian was eventually stopped in his driveway. The case is ongoing and has been referred to the district attorney.

The lettered panels decorating Yankee Stadium were auctioned off at Sotheby's with expectations of bringing in between $300k-$600k, however, only $260k was offered, leaving the current owner uninterested. Reggie Jackson, now aged 68, bought the letters for $260k in 2008 when Yankee Stadium was demolished as part of its renovation project. Jackson hopes that the next generations will continue enjoying the icons. Yankees fans and baseball enthusiasts eagerly anticipated the sale due to the significance of these letters in the history of both the sport and their city. The Mets also had a lot more money in their bid at $112,500 for a car from the early 1960s, which

Bob Shannon, a former boxer and trainer of Ricky Hatton, has been charged with sexually assaulting a girl under the age of 16. This occurred after police received a complaint about a reported sexual assault. Shannon trained Hatton for just one fight in November 2012, where Hatton lost to Vyacheslav Senchenko. Shannon is accused of engaging in inappropriate behavior toward victims of color. As a result, many members of the community were outraged at how Hatton's mentor handled what was clearly a situation requiring professional intervention.

Nabil Bentaleb is an important player for Tottenham Hotspur who signed him after he played excellently under Mauricio Pochettino. He has a new deal that would pay him millions per year which could lead to other clubs wanting him. The talk about this happening have made it clear that the contract should be finished quickly before other teams try and get him.

In an interview, former Liverpool and Blackburn manager Kenny Dalglish suggested that Harry Kane, who recently made his first appearance for England's national team, should not participate in the Under-21 European Championship but instead focus on his potential future as a full international player under Roy Hodgson. Dalglish believed that Kane had demonstrated his ability to compete at the highest level when facing some of the world's best defenders during a recent match, and that the young striker was ready to become a regular fixture in the England starting line-up. Dalglish also stated that Kane should avoid overexerting himself by participating in the Under-21 competition due to the possibility of tiring himself out. Tottenham would hope that their star striker does not put too much strain on himself while simultaneously competing for the

Kaka and Larin played crucial roles in Orlando City's victory over Portland Timbers. Kaka scored a penalty that secured the win, while Larin's quick reaction allowed Orlando to take the lead. Orlando also beat the Toronto FC last night, improving to 2 wins out of 5 matches.

An airport baggage handler in Seattle who was handling cargo mistakenly flew in another city due to falling asleep on the job has been caught filming himself doing pushups during his break. NBC journalist Jeff Rossen captured footage of the employee exercising on the runway after loading a plane. Despite performing 12 push ups without stopping, the employee remains dedicated to fitness despite not getting much rest. His hard work has even earned him recognition online.

Feyenoord forward Dirk Kuyt is set to return to the Dutch Eredivisi club he played for for nine years. The forward grew up there, played for the national team twice, and won multiple awards while playing for the Eredivisi club. He hopes to continue playing for the club when his one-year contract expires. Kuyt wants to play for the Netherlands again because the country has not been represented well in the recent World Cup. He says he can achieve greatness like the legendary Ryan Giggs if he plays for Feyenoord.

A truck crashed into a car on Interstate 93 in Boston this morning, causing hundreds of commuters to be delayed in their rush hour commute. Both drivers involved suffered minor injuries and needed to go to the hospital, while the truck was replaced with shuttle buses. Traffic was severely disrupted due to the accident, leading to further delays among those who attempted to catch their daily train ride.

Liverpool goalkeeper Simon Mignolet visited the cockpit of a plane while returning home from international duty with Belgium. Despite being part of the squad, he did not play due to injury concerns affecting Chelsea teammate Thibaut Courtois. Mignolet shared the experience of flying back to England via Facebook. Since joining the club mid-season, he has shown improved performance, including six consecutive Premier League clean sheet victories.

Mike Ashley has secured a 8.92% stake in Rangers through Sports Direct, while Ashley and Sports Direct have also taken a 75% grip of the Rangers Retail subsidiary. However, the Ibrox board is currently being led by interim chairman Paul Murray and director John Gilligan who are still working on finding an amicable way forward with Ashley. The Board is considering drawing down the second $5 million from Sports Direct as well as negotiating further deals such as borrowing money from the Three Bears consortium or selling assets to pay off Ashley's debt.

Daniel Sturridge, the Liverpool striker, was entertained by a mock imitation of Michael Owen during a promotion video for BT Sport. Sturridge found Owen's mimicry extremely amusing, particularly the similarities between them. However, despite being impressed by Owen's voice and movement, Farley struggled with his dancing performance, leaving much to be desired compared to Sturridge.

In March 2018, the U.S. Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals upheld the convictions of Clark Elmore, a Washington man who killed his 14-year-old stepdaughter in 1995. The court affirmed the Washington Supreme Court's rejection of Elmore's claim that his constitutional rights were violated during his trial. Elmore's case garnered significant attention due to the shocking nature of his actions and the severity of his crimes. Although Elmore's conviction and sentence remain unchanged, the decision of the appellate court means that he will no longer face execution, with Governor Jay Inslee having placed a moratorium on the death penalty. The ruling also prevents Elmore from challenging his conviction further through the U.S. Supreme Court.

Swansea City showed renewed interest in Blackburn Rovers striker Rudy Gestede, who has scored 20 goals this season and is valued at £7 million. However, other clubs like Marseille’s Andre Ayew remain targets due to higher transfer fees. Swansea City may consider Aleksandar Mitrovic, an Anderlecht player, or Obbi Oulare, a striker from Club Brugge. However, the deal with Gestede could be complicated by financial regulations imposed by Football Association Rules of Professional Football (FAR).

In early April, about $70,000 worth of bull semen was reportedly stolen from a farm in southern Minnesota. The stolen semen was taken from an unlocked barn and consisted of vials ranging from approximately $300 to $1,500. The thief is believed to be someone who purchases frozen bull semen as part of his business ventures. Bull semen is in demand due to its usefulness in reducing transportation costs associated with breeding cattle.

Wesley Burton, a popular radio host and father-of-three in California, was struck and killed by a car while driving home from work. His wife, Lucrecia, made an emotional plea for help in finding out what happened to him, and an ongoing effort by authorities and the community is trying to find out how this tragedy could have taken place. A GoFundMe account has also been set up to help cover his funeral costs and other expenses.

In this news article, Arsenal midfielder Serge Gnabry, who recently returned from a serious knee injury that had sidelined him for nearly a year, shared positive updates about his recovery process. Despite facing setbacks, Gnabry expressed optimism about his upcoming comeback and emphasized how much progress he's making towards returning to action with his team.

Bastian Schweinsteiger, one of Bayern Munich's key players, is doubtful for their upcoming German Cup match against Bayer Leverkusen due to an ankle injury he picked up while playing for Borussia Dortmund earlier in the week. The midfielder has had a challenging time recovering from numerous injuries throughout the season, including missing the opening four months of the campaign with a knee injury before returning in December. This latest setback could impact the team's plans as they aim to build momentum leading into their UEFA Champions League round robin phase.

On March 9th, Manchester City stars gathered with more than 100 school children at the academy stadium to unveil a giant mural featuring "The Football Effect". This project, designed by artists, was meant to inspire young people and promote inclusivity through football. Players like Vincent Kompany and Gael Clichy helped create it. The mural, which is over 64 meters long, features various forms of football such as night football, street football, and leagues for those with disabilities. It is located in the Academy Stadium, where Manchester City plays. The club hopes the mural will reach the wider public and help spread the message of its "Football Effect", which they have been working towards since last year. However, recent performance challenges, including a loss to Crystal Palace, may impact their

Two 12- and 13-year-old boys spray WD40 at another 12-year-old in a subway before threatening his life, according to police in England. One of them threatened to burn the boy with a lighter after he stopped for some food around Easter. Two suspects have reportedly been identified, though they haven't been formally charged.

Arsenal are reportedly set to sign 16-year-old Argentine defender Maxi Romero from Velez Sarsfield for around £4. 5 million. The youth sensation was described as the next Lionel Messi and has already completed an impressive stint with the first team. This news comes after Arsenal recently added Krystian Bielik to their squad via transfer in January.

Brazilian footballer Dani Alves has reportedly been offered a new contract extension by Barcelona as his current deal with the club is set to expire this summer. Several European superclubs including Manchester United and Liverpool remain interested in Alves despite rumors linking him with transfer talks. Football manager Javier Bordad discussed the matter with Alves' representative, Dinorah Santana, who confirmed that Alves was given a proposal to renew his contract with Barca. However, Santana declined to comment further about how Alves feels regarding the situation. Alves recently returned to play for Spain national team against Almería after missing previous matches due to injury issues. His return comes just weeks before his contract with Barcelona ends.

Two elderly Michigan parents' son-in-law and nephew pleaded guilty in federal court today in connection with a massive illegal weapons cache found hidden behind their house. The pair, Steven "Nick" Nickelbier and his sister, Sarah "Stella" Nickelbier, admitted to being part of an organized criminal enterprise known as the Southeast Michigan Volunteer Militia. Prosecutors allege the duo spent millions purchasing guns and other contraband, while also defrauding the elderly parents out of thousands of dollars through fake checks made payable to them from their pension funds. Nickelbiers are currently serving a sentence in prison.

In this shocking incident filmed by a Russian filmmaker, three people survived a catastrophic collision between a hot air balloon and a tall transmission tower that caused severe injuries and damages. Despite their minor injuries, the survivors were rushed to the hospital where they received medical treatment. The accident occurred when the balloon accidentally hit the tower at high speed, causing it to disintegrate along with the basket carrying three people. While the filmmakers captured the event via their mobile phone, bystanders quickly moved to assist those trapped inside.

In an interview with Esquire magazine, Conor McGregor, who is preparing for his world title fight against Jose Aldo, claimed that Floyd Mayweather lacks skills in areas beyond boxing. According to McGregor, if he were to fight Mayweather, "it would take me less than thirty seconds to wrap around him like a boa constrictor and strangle him." McGregor further stated that if Mayweather were to be boxed, he would easily defeat him. Mayweather is currently in training for his upcoming fight against Manny Pacquiao.

Dr. Alex Zhavoronkov, an anti-aging expert, predicts to live to 150 due to advancements in medicine and better lifestyle choices. He follows a strict regimen including exercise, medication, supplements, health checkups, and avoiding marriage, children, and material possessions, focusing solely on anti-aging research. Zhavoronkov's confidence stems from the belief that over time, these methods will significantly extend lifespan beyond current levels. His approach includes taking vaccinations early, closely monitoring biological parameters like blood chemistry and cell counts, engaging in rigorous physical activity, and adhering to conventional treatments.

Alvaro Negredo, the Spanish striker currently on loan at Valencia, may be sold to Manchester City if Valencia attempts to sell him. Valencia is reportedly considering a transfer, leading to concerns among City fans about their ability to complete the move without funds. Valencia's link with Radamel Falcao also looms large in the mix. City scouts have been observing Alex Sandro, the Portuguese center back for FC Porto, who helped lead Porto to a surprising Champions League victory.

Greg Rusedski supports Aljaz Bedene's bid to play for Great Britain in the Davis Cup despite the former's citizenship being pending. Bedene faces criticism for switching nationalities but believes his rise would encourage others to succeed. He's currently ranked 99th in the world and could debut as a British player in the upcoming AEGON Manchester Trophy. Rusedski hopes the decision would motivate other players below him. Bedene's case hinges on the International Tennis Federation allowing him to compete for Great Britain.
